intent: We need a new slice provisioned for the upcoming Formula E street race. Expect high crowd density and massive data consumption.
The port authority requires a dedicated URLLC slice for crane and autonomous vehicle coordination at Terminal 4.
Spin up a slice for the remote surgery pilot program at City General Hospital.
A new enterprise customer is onboarding next Monday and needs their private slice ready by Friday.
The airline's ground operations team requires isolated network connectivity for baggage tracking and gate management systems.
Provision a eMBB slice for the university campus covering dormitories and lecture halls.
Mining site in Zone 7 needs a slice for autonomous drill operations and real-time telemetry uplink.
Set up a temporary broadcast slice for the national election coverage team deployed at the capital this weekend.
The slice we stood up for the trade show expired yesterday; please decommission it and release the resources.
Shut down the emergency response slice that was activated during last week's flooding incident.
The construction company's contract ended. Their dedicated slice is no longer required.
Tear down slice NSI-884 and reclaim all associated RAN and core resources.
The pilot for remote inspection drones has been cancelled. Please remove the associated slice.
We're done with the Black Friday retail campaign slice. Decommission it as of midnight tonight.
The broadcast slice used for the marathon streaming coverage can be taken down now that the event has ended.
Pull up the current inventory of all deployed slice instances across the northern region.
Before the capacity review meeting, I need to see which slices are currently active on gNB cluster 12.
Can you dump the slice catalog including SST and SD identifiers for audit purposes?
How many slices do we currently have provisioned across all RAN sites in the eastern zone?
Show me all slices that are in an active operational state right now.
I need the full slice list, including which ones are associated with enterprise SLAs, before the quarterly review.
Retrieve all running network slice instances, no additional performance data needed for now.
The sports arena event starts in two hours and we're expecting three times more fans than originally planned. Raise the UE limit on that slice.
Due to higher-than-projected adoption, the smart meter slice needs to accommodate double the number of registered endpoints.
Bump the device cap on the logistics fleet slice. The client added 200 new delivery trucks to their network.
The public safety slice was configured for 500 devices but first responders are bringing 850 terminals to the drill.
Student enrollment went up by 40%. The campus mMTC slice can't handle that many endpoints at current config.
We sold an additional block of SIM cards to the warehouse operator. Update their slice's UE quota accordingly.
Reduce the UE quota on the test slice; we're moving those devices to production and don't need excess headroom.
PDU session throughput on the video surveillance slice is being capped. Raise the session limit before the city council meeting tomorrow.
The telemedicine slice is dropping sessions because the concurrent session ceiling was set too low during initial provisioning.
We're migrating more VPN clients onto the enterprise mobility slice. Adjust the PDU session quota to match.
Concurrent sessions on the connected vehicle slice are maxing out every morning during the rush period. That limit needs to go up.
Lower the allowed session count on the legacy M2M slice; most of those endpoints have been migrated and we want to prevent orphan sessions.
The drone delivery partner is scaling operations. Their slice's PDU session ceiling no longer matches the fleet size.
RAN throughput on the broadband slice is degraded. Can we increase the resource block share to offset the capacity crunch?
The live streaming service is complaining about buffering during evening hours. Give their slice a larger RB allocation during peak window.
Reduce the RB ratio on the IoT slice and redirect those radio resources to the eMBB slice where demand is higher.
Warehouse automation robots are experiencing latency spikes. Review and increase the RB share on the URLLC slice at Site 22.
After last night's tuning, the gaming slice still doesn't have enough radio bandwidth. Bump its RB ratio by another 10 percent.
The RAN resources for the education slice are oversized relative to actual usage. Trim the RB allocation and reallocate.
We need to cap how many simultaneous RRC connections are allowed for the consumer broadband slice during stadium overflow scenarios.
The gNB at the downtown hub is overloaded with RRC connections. Reduce the connected user limit for the non-priority slice groups.
Increase the RRC connection quota for the first responder slice on the three gNBs covering the disaster zone.
We're approaching the RRC signaling ceiling at Node B-11. Adjust the connection limits per slice group to ease the load.
The entertainment slice is causing signaling storms during mass events. Set a tighter RRC connection ceiling to prevent it.
We're seeing elevated MCS degradation on Sector 3 of gNB-201. Can you investigate whether it's interference-related?
There are multiple P1 alarms firing on the transport ring between Hub A and Hub B. What's the impact on live traffic?
Customers in the downtown district are reporting high latency on video calls. Is there a backhaul congestion issue?
Can you check whether the software upgrade on MME cluster 3 completed successfully last night?
